today_news.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
today_news.summary: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

today_news.title: Claude Sonnet 5 continues Anthropic's pattern of hiding price increases behind unchanged token rates
today_news.summary: Claude Sonnet 5 ranks fifth in the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index with 53 points and even beats the pricier Opus 4.8 on some agent-based tasks. But the model chews through about 40 percent more tokens per task than its predecessor, nearly doubling real costs despite identical list prices. An...

today_news.title: Meta's non-invasive brain-to-text AI is closing the gap with surgical implants
today_news.summary: Meta's FAIR AI team uses Brain2Qwerty v2 to translate brain activity into typed sentences, with no implants or surgery required. The system reads magnetic signals outside the skull and reconstructs what a person is typing. Clinical use for paralyzed patients is still a long way off, but accuracy kee...

today_news.title: Hidden code in Claude Code secretly flagged Chinese users
today_news.summary: Anthropic is removing a hidden monitoring feature from its programming tool, Claude Code, after it sparked outrage on social media.

today_news.title: The Download: AI “coworkers” and stratospheric internet

today_news.title: SpaceX shows investors a slim AI smartphone prototype powered by xAI technology
today_news.summary: SpaceX showed investors a prototype AI smartphone that's supposedly thinner than an iPhone and integrates xAI tech. The device runs on a Qualcomm Snapdragon chip with its own operating system. Musk wants to build an "everything app" modeled after WeChat.

today_news.title: Meta follows SpaceX's playbook and builds a cloud business to sell its spare AI compute to outside customers
today_news.summary: Meta is building its own cloud business to sell spare AI compute to outside customers. With planned AI investments of up to $145 billion this year alone, the same question that came up with xAI now applies to Meta: why isn't the company putting all that capacity to work on its own models?

today_tech.title: Do Multimodal Large Language Models Need Reasoning to Classify Dementia from Speech?
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have emerged as a promising approach for improving the accuracy, transferability, and explainability of automatic dementia classification (ADC) systems from voice recordings. Yet it remains unclear whether their reasoning capabilities are beneficial...

today_tech.title: From Objectives to Applications: Aligning Architectural Biases in Audio Self-Supervised Learning
today_tech.summary: Abstract: This paper examines audio self-supervised learning (SSL) through the alignment between pretraining objectives, architectural inductive biases, and downstream applications. Rather than treating SSL methods as a chronological sequence of pretext tasks or model families, we ask how different ...

today_tech.title: SemiScope: Disentangling Classifier Tuning and Joint Optimization in Semi-Supervised Security Classification
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Background. Labeled data for security classification is scarce. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) propagates labels from a small labeled pool to larger unlabeled pools. today_tech.title: Benchmarking Frontier LLMs on Arabic Cultural and Sociolinguistic Knowledge: A Cross-Evaluation Framework with Human SME Ground Truth
today_tech.summary: Abstract: The cost of human expert evaluation is a principal bottleneck to deploying language models in specialized, high-stakes domains.
